canonical_question: What are SaaS CAC payback period benchmarks by segment - when is it a red flag?
constraints: Requires fully-loaded S&M cost allocation — partial attribution produces misleading payback figures
Does not account for time value of money — a 12-month payback in a high-interest-rate environment is worse than it appears
Ignores churn — payback is meaningless if the customer churns before the payback period ends
Blended CAC masks channel-level economics — organic channels can subsidize an unprofitable paid channel
Enterprise segment benchmarks assume 95%+ net revenue retention; without it, longer payback periods destroy unit economics

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs overall SaaS unit economics or LTV:CAC ratio analysis
User needs to calculate or reduce customer acquisition cost itself
User is evaluating SaaS churn rates or net revenue retention

often_confused_with.label: SaaS CAC Benchmarks (raw acquisition cost, not payback time)

inputs_needed.question: What customer segment does the SaaS company primarily serve?
What is the company's current stage?
